title: Ability Record (v2 — methodology-first)
description: v2 REVERSAL: this schema serializes the Guidebooks design methodology (strategy-first, item-anchored — see Guidebooks/class_item_baselines.md and Guidebooks/elementalist_shaman_rework.md 'Ability Framework'), not the old Skill Creator record. The per-class Classes/2.0/*_Skills.yaml files are the CURRENT format this schema formalizes; Skills/GenerateWater.yaml-style thin records are PRE-METHODOLOGY and are the migration targets. The methodology core of every ability is: TriggerCondition (when is this the correct button) | ItemBaselineReplaced (which consumable this is the premium replacement for — 'items are the floor, classes are the ceiling') | DecisionCreated (the choice it forces) | CCStage (the CC budget contract) | RankProgression (Rank = capability, gear = numbers). The typed Effects vocabulary (Schemas/EffectTypes.yaml) survives as the nested MECHANICAL PACKET — the most downstream detail, not the design. Existing files use lowercase/snake_case spellings; validators normalize via the alias map in x-fieldAliases rather than forcing rewrites. Per DESIGN_OVERRIDE_SYSTEMS.md §25, identity-defining abilities are RESOURCE-GATED, never long-cooldown-gated: a Cooldown/RecastTime over 15s without a §25 marker is audited. A methodology field whose value is or starts with 'PENDING' counts as undecided (draft), mirroring the stat contract's PENDING coefficient state.

Key.description: Namespaced id — standalone Skills/*.yaml records only; per-class entries are identified by Name + file.

TriggerCondition.description: METHODOLOGY CORE. The situation that makes this the correct button ('Classes act when the SITUATION demands' — DESIGN_OVERRIDE.md §3). 'None (always available)' is a valid declared trigger.

ItemBaselineReplaced.description: METHODOLOGY CORE. Which item(s) a party without this class uses for the same strategic ground, and why this is the premium replacement (class_item_baselines.md). 'No item equivalent — <why>' is a valid declaration.

DecisionCreated.description: METHODOLOGY CORE. The player choice this ability forces — decision density is the game (Game Juice Taste Design.md, Position × Action × Time).

CCStage.description: METHODOLOGY CORE. CC budget contract: 0-3, null (no CC — an explicit declaration, keep the key), or a string for stance-variant cases ('Varies (Stealth=null, Ink=1, ...)').

RankProgression.description: METHODOLOGY CORE. Per-rank capability changes (rank_1..rank_4 / R1..R4 keys, or prose). Rank = capability, gear = numbers (DESIGN_OVERRIDE.md §2). 'PENDING — <note>' if the rank pass hasn't run.

CritCondition.description: Deterministic crit condition (crit is NEVER RNG — DESIGN_OVERRIDE.md §12). May instead live in a file-level crit section.

FailureState.description: What going wrong looks like — failure mode defined before success mode (DESIGN_LEARNINGS.md §9). Usually class/file-level; per-ability allowed.

ResourceCost.description: The §25 gate: what is spent (gauge/charges/state). Preferred over cooldowns for identity abilities.

Cooldown.description: Allowed for tactical utilities. >15s on an identity ability without a §25 marker ('§25 PASS/CHANGE/COMPLIANT' in the value or a section_25_note) gets audited.

Type.description: Free-form action kind as the class files use it (GCD/oGCD flag lives separately or in the string): 'Tentacle Grab (Stealth)', 'Stance Passive', 'Weaponskill', ...

Potency.description: Number, formula string ('150 + (Altitude × 5)'), per-weapon map, or null for pure utility.

Effects.description: The typed MECHANICAL PACKET — Schemas/EffectTypes.yaml vocabulary. Downstream serialization of the design, never a substitute for the methodology fields above.

Mechanics.description: v1 packet block — still valid; new records may flatten these fields to the top level instead.
